description_long: This bollard consists of a large, irregularly shaped stone base topped with a bright yellow plastic cone. The stone base is rough and natural-looking, with a grayish color and some visible weathering. The yellow cone is affixed to the top of the stone and features black circular markings and a sticker with a crossed-out symbol, likely indicating a warning or restriction. One side of the stone displays a round metallic plaque, suggesting it may serve a commemorative or informational purpose. The overall construction appears robust and heavy, making it suitable for controlling access or marking boundaries in urban environments. The combination of natural stone and high-visibility plastic makes it both functional and attention-grabbing.
description: A heavy gray stone base with a bright yellow plastic cone top, featuring warning markings and a metallic plaque on one side.
description_view_0: The front view shows the yellow cone with a warning sticker and the broad, rounded face of the stone base.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the curvature of the stone and the side of the yellow cone, with less visible detail on the stone.
description_view_2: This view reveals the metallic plaque embedded in the stone and the warning symbol on the cone.
description_view_3: The rear angle shows both the metallic plaque and the warning markings on the cone, with more of the stone's texture visible.
